title: Assignment & Increment 🚀
description: Shortcuts to write code faster: +=, ++, and --.

slides.title: Programmer Laziness 🥱
slides.content:
Good programmers are efficiently lazy.

Updating a variable based on its own value is incredibly common in programming (like adding points to a score). Writing `score = score + 10;` is too much typing for C developers.

slides.title: Compound Assignment 🎯
slides.content:
C gives us compound operators to combine Math and Assignment:

* `x += 5` instead of `x = x + 5`
* `y -= 2` instead of `y = y - 2`
* `z *= 10` instead of `z = z * 10`
* `a /= 2` instead of `a = a / 2`

quizData.question: If `int hp = 100;`, what is the value of `hp` after `hp -= 20;`?
quizData.explanation: `hp -= 20` translates perfectly to `hp = hp - 20`, bringing the health down to 80.

slides.title: Increment & Decrement ⬆️⬇️
slides.content:
Even `x += 1` is considered too long. If you just need to add or subtract ONE, you can use:

* **`x++`** (Increment): Adds 1 to x
* **`x--`** (Decrement): Subtracts 1 from x

slides.title: The Ultimate Shortcut
slides.content: You will use `++` and `--` non-stop when building loops later in the course.

quizData.question: To add exactly 1 to a variable called `score`, you can simply type `score`; followed by the _____ operator.
quizData.explanation: The `++` operator increments the variable by 1.
